audit_purpose: Independent execution of HeliX OSINT zero-day oracle with full chain-of-custody: code hash, config, outputs, and claim boundary.

what_this_run_shows: The pipeline ingests 4 fixture signals deterministically and produces a schema-valid artifact.
Correlation gating fires: 0 alerts because min_independent_sources=2 was not met within correlation_window_hours=24 in this fixture slice.
The noise_floor_report nonetheless surfaces the v8 component as a below-threshold observation with claim_level=needs_human_review, demonstrating the claim-ladder discipline at the artifact layer.
Every output caveat required by the oracle is present: 'not a confirmed vulnerability', 'no CVE or vendor advisory is asserted', 'human review required'.
what_this_run_does_not_show: This run does not prove zero-day predictive capability.
This run does not execute against live network sources (no GitHub / HackerNews / RSS fetch).
This run does not exercise the DeepInfra LLM path (llm_mode=synthetic).
This run does not compute lead_time_hours / precision / recall — those metrics live in the separate preregistered backtest (zero-day-osint-ground-truth-backtest).
A passing run with 0 alerts is consistent with either correct noise-floor gating or a fixture that is too thin to alert; it is not evidence of predictive power.

claim_boundary: Conservative OSINT correlation only. This audit run confirms the oracle executes deterministically end-to-end against a hashed fixture, produces the required caveats, and enforces the claim ladder; it does not confirm zero-day prediction.

chain_of_custody.note: Given the same code hashes and fixture hash, a re-run in fixture mode should reproduce the ingested counts and noise_floor_report structure. Absolute timings and generated_ms will differ.

reproduction_command: cd C:\Users\Big Duck\proyectos\helix-backend-repo && python tools\run_live_zero_day_oracle.py --fixture-only --llm-mode synthetic --output-dir <new-dir>
